$comment: Human-curated feed of notable additions to Gracchus. Append newest-first. Types: tranche | project | disclosure | coverage | dataset | feature. `view` is a Dashboard view id (matches VIEW_SLUGS keys); omit to render without a CTA.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — donor-supplier overlap surfacing live
entries.summary: Phase 3 now processes the full 6,819-record donations dataset (was: top-100 aggregate, which surfaced zero overlaps). Nine real overlaps now appear on the canvas — Deloitte LLP, Sir Robert McAlpine, William Cook Defence Systems, Canary Wharf Group, Heathrow Airport Holdings, Imagination Industries. Each is a tracked supplier whose firm also donated to a UK political party; the supplier bubble carries a coloured ring in the dominant funded party's colour. The supplier drawer now has a 'Political giving' section with per-party totals and donation date range. The Gracchus thesis — firms that hold government contracts also funding the party that gives them out — is finally legible at a glance.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — donors on the canvas + donor-supplier overlap
entries.summary: The top 25 political donors by aggregate £ (Electoral Commission record) now appear on the Money Map canvas, wired by donor→party edges in party colours. Where a tracked supplier is also on that donor list — Randox is the textbook case — the supplier bubble gains a coloured ring in the party's colour, signalling 'this firm both held government contracts and donated to the party in power'. The donor profile drawer cross-references contract and donation history side-by-side. Phase 3 of Money Map v2 — the full donor→party→minister→contract money loop is now visible.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — parties on the canvas
entries.summary: Adds political parties as a new node kind. Each person with a party affiliation now sits next to their party on the canvas, coloured in standard party colours. Click a party to see who's connected to it on this canvas, the regulator findings against its people, and the live proceedings still in play. Phase 3 will add donor-to-party edges from the Electoral Commission record.

entries.title: Money Map v2 — people on the canvas
entries.summary: The Money Map canvas now includes 19 people as a new node kind, wired from the connection records. Each person is linked to their counterparty (supplier or buyer) and the departments they served in. Click a person bubble to open a profile drawer with their roles, their connections, and the underlying source documents. Phase 2 will add political parties and donations as further node kinds.

entries.title: Connection catalogue expanded — 12 more stories
entries.summary: The Money Map Stories tab now spans Osborne (BlackRock), Clegg (Meta), Javid (JP Morgan), Hammond, Patel, May, Harding/Penrose (NHS Test & Trace), Zahawi (Balshore), Meller (PPE VIP lane), Uniserve, and Benton (lobbying suspension). Mix of regulator findings, ACOBA-approved appointments, and public-record entries. Sourced to gov.uk, parliament.uk, court records, and investigative journalism.

entries.title: Four more connection stories — Cameron, Crothers, Bourne, Cox
entries.summary: Second-wave individual-connection records from the revolving-door research brief. Cameron’s Greensill lobbying, Crothers’s dual civil-service / Greensill role, Alex Bourne’s Hancock-linked PPE contract, and Geoffrey Cox’s outside legal earnings. Sourced to Parliamentary records, Treasury Committee / PACAC findings, and investigative reporting where applicable.

entries.title: Money Map polish pass — 13 upgrades before ship
entries.summary: Shareable URLs (view/lens/tier/min/q), sticky filter bar, export-current-view-as-PNG, dashed strokes on undisclosed-£ bubbles, HHI/dependence qualitative bands (US DoJ guidelines), hover tooltips on every bubble, drawer focus trap + ARIA, collapsible mobile rails, repeat-offenders → supplier profile cross-link, weekly-rotating story cards, empty-canvas overlay with reset CTAs, discoverable Min £ cycler, and a shared useDrawerFocus hook.

entries.title: Drawer accessibility — focus trap, Esc close, empty states
entries.summary: Project, Supplier, and Buyer dossier drawers now trap focus, return it to the thing you opened them from, and close on Escape. Unresolved links render a terse 'unavailable' panel instead of crashing. Skip-to-content link wired for keyboard users.

entries.title: Story of the Week rotates weekly
entries.summary: The homepage lead cycles through the six most-overrun projects with disclosed primes, picking by ISO week-of-year so every reader sees the same pick in a given week. Current week's story is data-picked, not hand-curated.

entries.title: Citation chips in Money Map drawer + print styles
entries.summary: Money Map node drawer now exposes copy-citation chips on total-£, HHI, buyer/supplier counts, and top-edge figures. Dossiers print clean on light paper — header chrome hidden, drawer flows the full page, links append their URL.

entries.title: Citation chip on every headline figure
entries.summary: Hover any figure on the home page, in a project dossier, or in a supplier profile — a small © appears, click to copy a terse citation (finding, source file, access date). The whole tracker is now quotable without re-keying.

entries.title: Story of the Week — Universal Credit IT, 618% over
entries.summary: The home page now leads with a data-picked story: the tracked UK megaproject with the worst overrun % and a disclosed prime above £100m. Current pick: Universal Credit IT (£2.2bn → £15.8bn) — primes IBM, Accenture, DXC.

entries.title: Methodology page
entries.summary: An editorial one-pager explaining how Gracchus sources, grades, and reasons about the data — plus what we don’t claim.

entries.title: Tranche 11 — OCDS re-match
entries.summary: Re-ran the OCDS matcher against the grown cache. Fresh contract notices from Contracts Finder and Find a Tender folded into existing project dossiers.

entries.title: Supplier £ disclosure at 25.5%
entries.summary: Across all tracker projects, 236 of 924 named supplier positions now have a public £ figure attached — up from 21% pre-tranche-8.

entries.title: Supplier Rankings
entries.summary: League tables of repeat offenders, single-buyer dependencies, and buyer concentration. Every row traces back to the contracts behind it.

entries.title: Coverage Transparency page
entries.summary: Per-project disclosure broken down by source tier. Readers can see exactly where the remaining gaps are and how we plan to close them.

entries.title: Tranche 10 — description-gated dept-CSV match
entries.summary: Dept-CSV matcher now gated on shared description tokens to avoid supplier-name collisions. Unlocked £1.30bn of previously-dropped candidates.

entries.title: Department spend CSVs — MoJ, DHSC, Cabinet Office
entries.summary: Added monthly transparency CSVs for six more central departments. Backfilled to January 2022 where the publisher’s archive allows.

entries.title: Money Map — Lens mode
entries.summary: Click any entity to pivot the graph into an ego-network of its 1–2 hop neighbourhood. Makes cross-department concentration visible in one glance.
